$comment: Copy to switchboard.config.json and edit. switchboard.config.json is gitignored — nothing in src/ carries personal facts, they all live here. The SWITCHBOARD_HOME env var overrides `home`. Unknown keys (including every `$comment*` field below) are ignored by the loader, so this file is self-documenting. Every key the schema accepts is shown here with a safe placeholder.
$comment_stateDir: Operational state (db, scratch, sessions, logs, tmux config, learned rules). Defaults to <home>/switchboard. Set this OUTSIDE home when home is an iCloud/Dropbox-synced folder — a live SQLite WAL DB and git coordination scratch must NOT be sync-managed (corruption/eviction).
$comment_dbPath: SQLite state file. Defaults to <stateDir>/switchboard.db. Override only to relocate the database itself.

clients.$comment: Enable/disable each CLI and optionally pin its binary path (resolved from PATH when omitted). codex is optional for Claude-only installs, but REQUIRED for the `coordinate` (Claude-implements/Codex-reviews) flow.

signal.$comment: Stays disabled until you register a dedicated number with signal-cli. `account` is that registered number (E.164); `allowlist` is the ONLY senders allowed to command the system (Invariant 2). See the README Prerequisites for how to OBTAIN a dedicated number (Twilio / Google Voice / second SIM).

dashboard.$comment: Localhost-bound control plane (it can kill sessions, decide approvals, spawn sandboxed sessions). `token` is a bearer token required on every /api route. It is MANDATORY — the server refuses to start without one, because an unauthenticated localhost service is CSRF-able by any web page you visit. `init` generates one; then open the dashboard once as https://<host>/?token=<token> (cross-site requests are also blocked). Reach it via `tailscale serve` on your tailnet, NEVER a public port and NEVER `tailscale funnel`; any tailnet device with the token is a full operator. Set allowInsecureNoToken:true ONLY for throwaway local dev.

tailscale.$comment: The macOS GUI app ships the CLI at the binPath below (not on PATH). `serve` exposes the dashboard on your tailnet only.

tmux.$comment: `socket` is the tmux server name (tmux -L <socket>). Leave the default unless you run a SECOND Switchboard profile on this machine (e.g. switchboard.config.test.json) — it MUST use a distinct socket so it never shares, reconciles against, or kills the production tmux panes.

approvals.$comment: How long a blocking tool call waits for an out-of-band (Signal/dashboard) decision, and the fail-closed default (an unanswered approval denies).

retention.$comment: Purge TERMINAL sessions (done/failed/killed) older than sessionDays, with their scratch/transcript/steering rows. The append-only audit_log is always preserved. Active sessions are never aged out. Set 0 to disable retention.

attach.$comment: Remote phone/laptop attach to interactive sessions. `sshHost` is your Mac over the tailnet (e.g. user@host.tailnet.ts.net); unset → attach emits only a bare LOCAL tmux command. `transport` mosh survives phone sleep/roaming (needs mosh-server); use ssh for clients without mosh (Termius, JuiceSSH, Windows). `promptFavorite` is only for Panic's Prompt app; other terminals use the copyable connection string. See the README terminal matrix.

policy.$comment: Overrides for the default permission matrix (keys are action names like write_outside_workdir, delete, network_egress → allow|deny|ask) and a global egress allowlist applied to all sessions.

ironcurtain.$comment: OPTIONAL, EXPERIMENTAL Docker-sandboxed execution backend (provos/IronCurtain). Leave enabled:false unless you have IronCurtain + Docker installed. MUST run on node@24 — node 26 SILENTLY disables the V8 sandbox (security-critical). See docs/IRONCURTAIN.md before enabling.

assets.$comment: Named template/asset paths your deliverable tasks reference, by short name → path.

repos.$comment: Short name → absolute path for repos you launch interactive sessions in.

$comment_codeRoots: Roots searched (2 levels deep) to resolve a project by name when it isn't listed in `repos`.

paths.$comment: Named output paths. driveOutput is a synced cloud folder for deliverables (writing there = ask).
